rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF LORETTA ASH
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Compliance Status Hearing
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The conservator must have sent a copy of the corrected Order Appointing Probate Conservator and an information notice about Loretta's rights (Form GC-341) to the conservatee, her attorney, and her relatives. Appointing Probate Conservator plus an information notice of the rights of conservatee [Form GC-341] must be attached to the order. Page 2 of GC-341 must be completed to file the form. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF JENNY LEA BROWNSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/9/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: 1. A death certificate was filed on 9/17/25. This is not a complete 13100 affidavit. A complete 13100 must be filed. It must include all of the language required by Probate Code Section 13101, including 13101(a)(5) and must also have attached to it the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-300, as required by Probate Code Section 13101(f). 2. The Judicial Council form Summary of Account has not been filed yet. 3. Other issues noted in the hearing notes for the hearing on 8/12/25 have not been addressed (date(s) of order(s) authorizing fees; incorrect prayer; attorney fees and costs). Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. Deadline is 11/24/25 for providing courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EDDIE MAE LUCKETT
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The proof of service filed on 8/5/25 was, again, not of the mandatory Judicial Council form Notice of Hearing (DE-120) required for Probate hearings with the instructions for how to appear remotely attached to the notice, and it shows that it was notice of just the "Frist Supplement," not of the petition. 2) No proposed order has been lodged or submitted. 3) No proof of bond has been filed. 4) No petition for final distribution has been filed. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MERVYN WYNN MEMORIAL TRAVEL FUND
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #15, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/25/2025. Issues necessitating a continuance: 1) The ending balance of cash assets in accounts must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. The statements or letters must encompass, not precede, the ending date of the accounting period (LRSF 14.34.D). 2) Fee requests must be supported by a declaration under penalty of perjury of services performed, time expended, average hourly rate, results accomplished, and benefit to the entity and must be supported by time sheets. Each timesheet entry must identify the attorney/paralegal providing the service and include the hourly rate for the attorney/paralegal (LRSF 14.53). Please address the above issues and provide courtesy copies of all further filings to this examiner no later than November 14, 2025, to avoid additional continuance.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MONICA BROWN ANDREWS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/10/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The "supplement" e-filed on 10/1/25 amended the petition (increased the request for statutory compensation). The caption is incorrect. [CRC 7.3.] (2) An amendment requires the same notice as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53(a).] Notice of Hearing of the petition and amendment (after the amendment has been e-filed) must be mailed at least 15 days before the 12/10/25 hearing. (3) Please file a copy of the trust or the pertinent portions thereof, supporting Executor's assertion that the trust provides for distribution to the minors via their mother. (4) How old are the minors? (5) A detailed schedule of gains (or losses) on sale must show the carry value and sale price of each asset, not a lump sum. Please provide a revised proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 11/24/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate and distribute an estate must begin with the following language: "ORDER FOR FINAL DISTRIBUTION." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] All pleadings must also show the name and representative capacity, if any, of the party for whom the attorney is appearing, e.g., John Jones, Executor. "Petitioner" or "Respondent" alone is not sufficient. [LRSF 14.15 A.])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALBERT COURTNEY HAYDEN IV
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/10/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The petition caption is incomplete if IAEA authority is being requested. (2) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. (3) Only the first box that applies should be checked in item 6. (4) Paragraph 5 of Attachment 2 is confusing. If this Court determines that no probate is necessary in California, how can there be an ancillary proceeding in Washington, DC? If Petitioner has an order for final distribution from Alameda County for John T. Hayden's estate, does that not suffice to give him standing in the estate of Ethel C. Hayden...? (5) If proceeding with this petition, please file a copy of the order for final distribution from Alameda County. (6) Exhibit A to Attachment 3g(2)(a) is just the petition filed in the District of Columbia, not the order appointing Petitioner as the successor personal representative of Ethel C. Haden's estate, and it does not appear to have been filed yet. (7) That petition indicates that the prior personal representative died on 10/9/24, not 10/10/24, as shown in this petition. (8) Who are the ultimate beneficiaries under the John T. Hayden Revocable Trust? Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 11/24/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
